title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Browser and Node Client Systems · TypeScript Build Room
summary: An accessibility product team integrating a staging browser API must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for cookies, credential modes, api keys, bearer tokens, and secret hygiene, producing least-authority credential and session policy. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch least-authority credential and session policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cookie Jar Scope
steps.instruction:
workshop case — accessible staging dashboard SDK handles host-only session cookie SID-3, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cookie Jar Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, secure, httponly, and samesite scope through a deliberate jar policy. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cookie Jar Scope, keep Cookie Jar Scope connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
